title: dianthus × saxatilis nothosubsp. varians
intro: Dianthus × saxatilis nothosubsp. varians, also known as the pink-pink or mountain pink, is a nothosubspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine where its roots and leaves are used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus × saxatilis nothosubsp. varians include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from digestive issues to skin conditions.
